seoTitle: Set Time Limits and Deadlines in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Add a countdown timer, schedule a start and end date, or insert a date question in Microsoft Forms to manage timed quizzes and deadlines.
intro: Timed exams and hard submission deadlines need more than trust in the honor system. Microsoft Forms includes built-in time controls that let you enforce a countdown clock on a quiz, schedule when a form opens and closes, or simply collect a timestamp as an answer.

steps.heading: Open form settings
steps.body: Click the three-dot More Form Settings icon in the top-right corner and select Settings.

steps.heading: Enable a quiz timer
steps.body: Check the Set time limit box and enter your preferred duration, such as 30 minutes, to trigger a live countdown clock.

steps.heading: Schedule open and close dates
steps.body: In the options for responses section, toggle on Start Date and End Date, then use the calendar picker to set exact opening and closing times.

steps.heading: Insert a Date question
steps.body: Return to the canvas, click Add New, and choose the Date question type if you want participants to submit a specific timestamp.

steps.heading: Preview the countdown
steps.body: Use the Preview button to confirm the countdown clock displays and behaves correctly on both desktop and mobile.

faq.question: Does the timer automatically submit the quiz when time runs out?
faq.answer: Yes, a live countdown clock is designed to trigger an automatic submission once the time limit expires.

faq.question: Can I set a form to open in the future automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, the Start Date option lets you schedule exactly when a form becomes accessible.

faq.question: Is the time limit different from the start and end date settings?
faq.answer: Yes, the time limit controls how long an individual respondent has once they begin, while start and end dates control the overall availability window.

faq.question: Can I collect a timestamp without setting a time limit?
faq.answer: Yes, adding a Date question lets you gather a specific date and time answer independently of the quiz timer.
